title: Bridge Essay: Behavioral, Neural, and Computational Principles of Bodily Self-Consciousness.
concept: Body as constructed percept — multisensory integration, rubber hand illusion, ownership plasticity.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Physical De-solidification** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Body illusion — multisensory integration, rubber hand illusion, body ownership as constructed percept.
**Phase context:** Body as constructed percept — multisensory integration, rubber hand illusion, ownership plasticity.

**Behavioral, Neural, and Computational Principles of Bodily Self-Consciousness.** — Neuron (2015). DOI: 10.1016/j.neuron.2015.09.029. PMID: 26447578. Recent work in human cognitive neuroscience has linked self-consciousness to the processing of multisensory bodily signals (bodily self-consciousness [BSC]) in fronto-parietal cortex and more posterior temporo-parietal regions. We highlight the behavioral, neurophysiological, neuroimaging, and computational laws that subtend BSC in humans and non-human primates.
